w:r.w:t: 6586185_DRV, 6WM, LHD, SAB, ARMREST, LUMBAR, HEATER, FABRIC CURITIBA BITON VP NAFTA
Test Requirements of Dynamic Test / Impact With Dummy / Generic
Test performed according to the Stellantis test procedure B32-3210
Test applicable on K0 complete front seat
K0 front seat crash fixture for metal validation (simulating podium as rigid)
STM file appendix 1 generic pulse for front impact (all seat rows) seat sizing generic pulse:
Description how to mount the fixture + Bpilar + foot rest on the sled (example of LH seat):
Method how to calculate the pelvis H-point by tracking the femur:
Initial position of the thigh model:
Maximum forward position of the thigh model:
Adjust the dummy in order to fullfill Hpoint and pelvis angle corridor.
Attach on the femur plate the targets P and F in order that Hpoint + target P + Target F are on one line
Measure the distance A between the H point and P femur pattern and angle alpha by recording with faro arm the Hpoint and target P and F coordinates
Measure on the first frame of the movie the angle Alpha formed by the straight line passing through the center of two femur patterns P and F and horizontal.
Measure at the maximum advanced position of the pelvis the angle Beta formed by the straight line passing through the center of the two femur patterns P and F and horizontal.
Measuring ΔXp at advanced X position of the point P
Measuring ΔZp at advanced Z position of the point P
Calculate the coordinates of H advanced such as:
1 - Mid Left 90° | 2 - Mid Right 90° | 3 - Top camera | 4 - Front Left 45°
1 - Measure with faro arm the buckle fixing point coordinates X / Y / Z

2 - At he buckle area, mark 15 points with interval of 10mm each on the cushion side-member and measure this points with faro arm the X / Y / Z coordinates as described in the test preparation section
3 - Measure with faro arm the X-Y-Z coordinates at the IB and OB FSC side-member nose area
4 - Measure the unlocking force at the buckle
1- Mark the belt every 100mm from the D-loop to the retractor and further
2 - Mark all the mechanisms with a white marker
3 - Fix markers like described under "Additional Information Video Analyze" and measure Y-distance to the camera
4 - At the buckle area, mark 15 points with interval of 10mm each on the cushion side-member and measure this points with faro arm
1 - Measure with faro arm the buckle fixing point coordinates X / Y / Z and calculate gap to pre-test

2 - At he buckle area, mark 15 points with interval of 10mm each on the cushion side-member and measure this points with faro arm the X / Y / Z coordinates as described in the test preparation section and calculate gap to pre-test
3 - Measure with faro arm the X-Y-Z coordinates at the IB and OB FSC side-member nose area and calculate gap to pre-test
4 - Measure the unlocking force at the buckle and calculate gap to pre-test
5 - Take picture of the lap belt if still in contact with the Iliac bone area for submarining rating
1 - Pelvis displacement in X and Z Video analysis (Refer to "Sketch / Picture Test Setup") + Double integration | 2 - Headrest centre line x-tracking without untrimming headrest
Add the following targets on the dummy and measure the distance to the camera : 12 targets totally to be recorded
Add the following targets on the seat and measure the distance to the camera : 13 Targets totally to be recorded
=> Top of the back frame IB / OB as per below instruction (untrim locally to fix it on the frame) :
=> Recliner pivot point IB / OB (remove the OB side valence if necessary)
=> Cushion side-member nose area as per below description:
PV_K0_NAFTA_DRV_ID 030 6586185_DRV, 6WM, LHD, SAB, ARMREST, LUMBAR, HEATER, FABRIC CURITIBA BITON VP NAFTA
(DX to be provided by the VE
(DZ to be provided by the VE)
Heel Point at the foot rest [mm]
Description how to adjust heel point coordinates:
To measure and to record with faro arm the floor support intersection point and real dummy heel point
1 - Original belt buckle for K0 project NAFTA variant
2 - Original PLP for K0 project NAFTA variant
3 - Original belt for K0 project with 600daN NAFTA variant
1 - Diagonal belt sensor => Distance to the D-ring = 200mm | 2 - Lap belt sensor outboard => Minimum distance to the outboard low fixation = 150mm
The PLP device shall be blocked (integrated in the Bpilar design)
Pull in force = (input to be filled by FEA)
Pull in displacement = (input to be filled by FEA)
110mm forward from the fully rearward position
For locking the tracks proceed to the below steps:
1 - Fully rearward to put the screw IN
2 - Fully forward to put the screw IN
3 - Driving minimum 4 times between the end stops fully forward and fully rearward
4 - Sliding the to the test position until locking
6 - Checking locking pin and mark it with white marker
Backrest Angle (describe exactly how to measure)
Backrest to be adjusted in design position => angle from recliner pivot point to headrest bezel exit centre point = 14.5°
1 - Adjusted to the height of the head => Z-backset closest to 0mm
2 - Adjust the headrest in X to the fully rearward position
Original Renault / Nissan screw and reducing head diameter to 17.8mm as per original screw drawing | | Torque = 35Nm for each of the 4 screws
SMP-917434 / PV_K0_NAFTA_DRV_ID 030 (Qty. 1) 6586185_DRV, 6WM, LHD, SAB, ARMREST, LUMBAR, HEATER, FABRIC CURITIBA BITON VP NAFTA
Cushion flange IB side-member permanent deformation in Y-direction
FSC side-member cushion nose area permanent deformation in Y-direction IB and OB sides
FSC side-member cushion nose area permanent deformation in Z-direction IB and OB sides
Belt buckle fixing point permanent deformation resultant vector X / Y / Z directions
Pelvis dynamic X-displacement IB and OB sides
Shall be less than front seat structure front edge and with 50mm margin
1 - determine initial DX distance between initial Hpoint and seat frame cushion nose area (both recorded with faro arm)
2 - Determine max dynamic Pelvis H-point outboard side = B
3 - Rating shall be : DX - B -50 > 0mm (same logic for IB side)
Seat adjustment and locking systems (headrest, backrest and track) maintain original set position
No portion of seat shall separate from integrated seat frame assembly
No aggressive part shall appear (sharp or cutting edge) which can be in contact with the dummy
The seat shall remain attached at each fixation point
No crack, no rupture, no breakage, no ejection, no separation of the seat or seat parts
No unlocking, adjusting or disengagement of any mechanism during the test
No separation or breakage of any part in such a way that tips or sharp edges could increase the risk of injury for the occupants
Max dynamic pelvis X-displacement average of IB and OB sides
Max dynamic pelvis Z-displacement downwards average of IB and OB sides
No submarining shall occur (by analysis positioning of the lap belt post test and checking lap belt force graph)
Dynamic X-displacement of the headrest using TOP target
Variation pre-post test unlocking forces at the buckle
General tolerance definition for Testing Europe, if not defined otherwise or not accurate enough for the test method. /
Generelle Toleranz Festlegung für Testing Europa, wenn nicht anders definiert oder nicht ausreichend genau für die Versuchsmethode.
Angle (e.g. seat adjustment, dummy position, reproducing the car environmental) /
Winkel (z.B. Sitzeinstellung, Dummypositionierung, Reproduktion der Fahrzeugumgebung)
Length / displacement e.g. seat adjustment, dummy position, reproducing the car environmental) /
Länge / Weg (z.B. Sitzeinstellung, Dummypositionierung, Reproduktion der Fahrzeugumgebung)
Torque (e.g. seat fixation / add on parts) /
relatively to the recliner pivot pin:
